entry.title: Re: Offer Letter Template Changes Not Reflecting After Sa...
entry.content:
Hi @Srivats , hope you're doing good.

If this is an error related to the release, then my suggestion is to check the main KBA where the known issues for 1H 2025 release are reported, updated by the Product Support Team on a regular basis:

2692971 - SAP SuccessFactors Known Issues for the 1H 2025 Release

In case your issue is not listed in any of the topics of this KBA, then please consider create a Product support ticket, attaching a documentation with the steps performed and its evidences, comparing if possible a scenario that is OK in Production, where the release is not available yet, and the scenario where the issue occurs in your Preview instance. This will speed up the analysis and response time of PS Team.

Best Regards.

Thiago Eva

entry.title: Re: failing to install ABAP Development Tools ADT - items could not be found
entry.content: Hi Vlad_Ghitulescu, This worked for me as well. I changed the Network Connections from Native to manual and ADT got updated. Thanks for your valuable Info!

entry.title: Re: Multitenant SaaS apps on SAP BTP using CAP? Tried-and-True! – The Expert Features
entry.content:
Hi @martinfrick,

Brilliant post series! Thanks for the detailed explanations on such complex and super useful functionalities!

I have a few questions that you may be be able to clarify:

1. Since currently apps can be federated and consumed from BTP Workzone, does that mean that a customer could consume the federated SaaS app FROM within their usual Workzone Portal (aka from their launchpad within their own customer global account) and hence the multitenant app could "inherit" their own custom theme (if any was applied to their workzone portal) without the partner having to enable the theme designer within our partner-global-account->customer-subscriber-subaccount, hence avoiding having to manage the custom theme from our consumer-subaccount?

2. One of the topics covered is how to push data from the ERP to the multi-tenant app, do you have any examples on the opposite? Aka, consuming standard released APIs from S4 (customer's ERP on-prem/public-cloud/private-cloud) or consuming events triggered from these customer's systems?

3. Do you have any information or timeline in which the feature-toggles would be ready for production use? It sounds really promissing, but the official documentation still indicates that it's not usable in productive deployments.

Best regards,

Lidia

entry.title: Re: ASP.NET Report Viewer & multiple IIS Worker Processes
entry.content: Thanks Don, I will try the suggestion. For some reasons, it will be 10-14 days before I am able to report back. Many thanks

entry.title: Collaborative Product Data Management for Discrete Industries
entry.content:
Recently, we published a blog (link) discussing how the SAP Business Suite provides a comprehensive system of record for managing all product data for an organization. A product data management strategy that leverages the integrated and innovative capabilities of SAP Business Suite provides unmatched value across your entire supply chain.

The ability to prioritize collaboration, compliance, and sustainability, while providing the product data backbone for the entire organization, transforms how companies unlock the value of unified product information in process and discrete industries.

To support our customer needs, the SAP strategy for our solutions is centered around customer choice and within the product lifecycle management market category, we will have the following focus areas:

  • Product Data Management as part of our Business Suite and
  • Integration with external PLM and CAD systems

In this blog we will now look at how the SAP Business Suite becomes the product data backbone for discrete industry customers. In further blogs we will also talk about integrating third party systems as well as how to manage product data management in process industries.

Understanding the Landscape of Discrete Industries

Customers in discrete industries have needs to not only manage and control product data that defines their products, but also to directly integrate their authoring tool systems (MCAD and ECAD). Customers are looking to also streamline the transition from Engineering BOM to Manufacturing BOM and fosters the ability to readily collaborate on the product related data between design, sourcing and manufacturing.

When organizations can release initial designs and incorporate design changes effortlessly – production readiness is enhanced, rework is reduced, and supply chain efficiency is boosted!

The Challenge of Product Data Creation

The main challenge for customers starts with getting an accurate product definition out of engineering and to have it readily available when the rest of the organization needs it to complete their launch readiness tasks (costing, sourcing, quality planning, manufacturing planning, etc.). When we consider the complexity of product designs these days – this is not a task for the faint of heart!

The challenge often starts because the product data management capabilities within organizations are lacking the direct connectivity to their ERP system and as well the needed sophistication to support rapid time to market initiatives because data is being stored on network drives and managed by paper-based processes which causes delays and data inaccuracy.

To address this challenge, SAP offers capabilities that seamlessly connect to a wide array of MCAD and ECAD authoring tools. This direct integration allows the SAP Business Suite to become the product data management solution for an organization. CAD files and their structures are easily stored and managed with full version and design history management along the evolution of the product definition. All of this is made available from the comfort of the designer’s workspace within their authoring tool. Providing ultimate flexibility and efficiency that yields maximum results for all parties involved!

Not only does SAP provide the ability to manage the CAD files and structures, but it facilitates the creation of the master data within the SAP Business Suite that is needed by the rest of the organizations (i.e., material masters, bills of materials, etc.) while keeping it in sync with the original CAD definition – creating the digital thread in SAP right from the start and available to everyone!

These structures can be baselined as the design evolves allowing for the ability to explode the versioned product structures according to the various validity dates and original baseline. When coupled with natively integrated visualization, the product developer can easily visually navigate the structure along its lifecycle – greatly reducing the time and effort to analyze the product definition and decision-making processes.

Product Development doesn’t happen in a vacuum

Product centric companies need enterprise collaboration with design or sourcing enterprise collaboration with all relevant information, insights and stakeholders to accelerate product development. As a product developer, I want to collaborate with team members and business partners to exchange and collaborate on product details (documents, specifications, bills of materials, etc.) to reduce lifecycle cost. SAP provides the ability to easily share this information in a secure and workflow driven workspace that encourages the visual review of information and the ability to easily incorporate information back into the product definition. As technical buyer or seller, I want to exchange product data with suppliers and vendors to "exchange" product data for sourcing related processes.

When design partners, contract manufacturers and buyers work on the right version of the product information coming from the SAP Business Suite, unnecessary rework and delays can be avoided. Organizations can increase productivity and save costs through establishing standardized business processes across the supply chain. Operational excellence can be achieved through reduction of manual data input by collaborating directly on structured product data.

Now let’s build it

Once design is complete, the product design needs to be made available to the supply chain for manufacturing. SAP streamlines the transition from Engineering BOM to Manufacturing BOM. Through tight collaboration between design and manufacturing, the initial release of information into production and the incorporation of design changes is effortless because the product data is already within the SAP Business Suite. This enhances an organization’s production readiness — while reducing rework and boosting efficiency.

When we build the seamless bridge between engineering and manufacturing with the SAP Business Suite, we foster better product quality, stay consistent even when frequent changes appear and enable the reuse of engineering data for manufacturing – enabling us to be quicker to market when launching new products.

Revolutionizing Design to Production

SAP revolutionizes the design to production process, offering advanced solutions tailored to enhance product development processes in discrete industries. By leveraging SAP’s product data management capabilities, companies can overcome the challenges faced in optimizing product development through utilizing the full native capabilities of the SAP Business Suite across the supply chain with its seamless connectivity with compliance, sustainability, and network functionalities. This comprehensive integration facilitates not only faster time to market, but also provides the ability to make real-time and informed decision and to align processes with broader business strategies based on a solid product data foundation.

The Road Ahead

As we advance into the future, the landscape of discrete industries will undoubtably continue to evolve, driven by innovation that will present new challenges and opportunities. SAP stands at the forefront, preparing companies with the necessary tools to navigate and lead this transformation. Our focus for these customers is to continue to enhance essential product data management capabilities within the SAP Business Suite and to extend these capabilities beyond the SAP Business Suite with differentiating capabilities, such as collaboration, product structure management, product handover and others, all while being network aware.

Leveraging the SAP Business Suite as your product data management solution fosters not only the efficient sharing of product data across the value chain but also supports the bi-directional change management process critical for all organizations need to get to market faster with new innovations.

To learn more about our strategy, solution capabilities, and future collaborative product data management innovations, please visit the following links:

  • Strategy blog: SAP Business Suite – The Integrated Powerhouse for Seamless Product Lifecycle Management
  • SAP website: Solutions for product lifecycle management
  • SAP Road Map Explorer: Innovations for collaborative product development

entry.title: Incorrect formatting of Decimals in CDS View
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are having a Node.js CAP application connected to HANA Cloud DB.

There is a view that fetches some information from the table, it is exposed as an OData service as per the standard CAP functionality.
We've noticed that after the CDS npm package version was upgraded to version 8 - view attributes with type "Decimal" are now returned as Strings and formatted as in the screenshot below:

CDS Version 6.8.4:

CDS Version 8.5.0:

Calling the view directly in the database returns correct results( DECIMAL value).
No changes were done to the view, and no transformations are applied to the returned value, only version of the cds package had changed.

Has anybody else encountered similar issues? Perhaps this is a side effect of some changes in cds package/ CAP framework?
Any assistance would be appreciated 🙂

Best regards,
Alex

entry.title: Re: Release Date of BI Platform 4.3 SP05
entry.content:
Hi

SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform 4.3 SP05 Maintenance Delivery
16.06.2025 – 20.06.2025

entry.title: Re: I need to add currency in drop down list for Job requ...
entry.content:
Hi @HeenaG ,

Based on the images you've attached, it seems that the required field  "Field Label" is missing for the configured filters, and that's probably the reason of the error message . Please check what should be the label for them and update them accordingly.

This Standard Filter error might be related with some new Recruiting Template validation.

Best Regards.

Thiago Eva

entry.title: Missing position flows for withholding tax.
entry.content:
How to configure position flows of withholding tax.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Missing position flows for withholding tax.
entry.content:
In the configuration activity 'Specify Flow Attributes for Extended Withholding Tax', the update type must be configured to have position flows of withholding tax.

Issue is also described in sap kba https://me.sap.com/notes/3596848

entry.title: Re: Data Migration material master data in japanese test...
entry.content:
Hi Eric,

Kindly open the template file using notepad++ and check the details provided are in the correct format or changed format. If the provided data is in different format it causes inconsistencies.

Also please refer to the below KBA.

  • 2692715 - Migration Cockpit: How to correctly fill data into XML template

Best regards,
Latha

entry.title: Transaction REISBDCT error, Field symbol has not been assigned yet.
entry.content: When I call the transaction "REISBDCT - Fixtures/Fittings for Master Data", I get the error Field symbol has not been assigned yet.

entry.title: Re: SDMI CL_SDM_BUT0BK_V2
entry.content:
Hi together,

 

i solved my issue and si-check is passed now.

First i implemented both notes as LEE325 already said.

Then i executed report "R_SDM_ONLINE_EXEC" for SDM class "CL_SDM_BUT0BK_V2" but unchecked "CHECK for qualification". Then the class is executed even it is not found in "sdm_mon". I did this for productive client and 000 client.

Maybe you even don´t neet the two notes - just the execution of the report with "unchecked for qualification"

May that helps 🙂

 

Best regards

Michael

entry.title: Re: Russia bank account number validations
entry.content: Hi Loren, could you please share how this issue was resolved on your end. Any help would be much appreciated as we are experiencing the same issue.

entry.title: How to avoid a purchase requisition for a material on stock
entry.content:
Dear community,

we are using app F1639A - My purchase requisitions to manually create P/Rs for non production material. In many cases existing material on stock in the warehouse will be requested. This creates unnecessary efforts in procurement to recognize the material on stock or will result in superfluous material in the warehouse. 

From a process point of view users should first look into the app F1076 Stock - Single Material, but this is usually not done.

Will it be possible to display the Unrestricted-Use Stock in the app F1636A next to the Quantity Requested? How can I do this? I did not find any possibility in the adapt UI for the app.

Or even better, will it be possible to start an ATP check to show the material on stock or delivery time similar to the ATP check in a sales quotation?

Thank you.

Best regards,

Dirk

entry.title: Re: How do I un-do a previously selected "Always Use Selected Format" choice?
entry.content: You can use the command &RESET_EXCEL like a transaction in command field:

entry.title: Replicate OData Service into ICF-Node with new Namespace
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to add a new OData service in maint_services to my gateway by replication from a different system.

Seems like they will land into opu/odata/sap/* 

How do I configure a new namspace or select a different node to replicate into?

Best,
Yascha

entry.title: Re: NOT ABLE TO POST PO FOR NLAG MATERIALS
entry.content:
Dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If you were previously able to post POs for this NLAG material, and now it's failing:

  • Someone may have changed the material master and removed or didn’t maintain the Plant Data/Storage Location view for plant 3330.
  • It could also be that the material was previously extended to 3330 but now it's not maintained properly.

 

Recommended Solution

You (or your MM team) should:

  1. Go to transaction MM02 (if using GUI) or use the “Change Material” app in Fiori.
  2. Enter the material number and plant 3330.
  3. Make sure the “Plant Data / Storage 1” and “Accounting” views are maintained.
  4. Also, confirm that:
    • The Material Type is NLAG (non-stock).
    • The Valuation Class, Price, and Procurement Type are set correctly.

NLAG materials (Non-Stock items) should normally not require Inventory Management, but some PO types or account assignment categories might still check for plant-specific data, depending on configuration.

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Tracking Eco-Friendly Practices with SAP Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS)
entry.content:
The garden furniture industry is blooming, but so is consumer scrutiny. For garden retailers, this means sourcing responsibly, minimizing environmental impact and proving their commitment to green practices. Enter SAP Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS)—a powerhouse solution for tracking and optimizing sustainability across the supply chain.

1. Ethical Material Sourcing: From Forest to Patio

Garden furniture relies heavily on materials like wood, metal, and textiles. However, unethical sourcing (e.g., deforestation or non-recycled plastics) can tarnish a brand’s reputation. SAP EHS tackles this by:

  • Certification Tracking: Automatically validating supplier certifications (e.g., FSC, Rainforest Alliance) to ensure compliance with sustainability standards.
  • Supplier Scorecards: Rating vendors based on eco-friendly practices, such as using low-VOC finishes or recycled aluminum.
  • Risk Alerts: Flagging suppliers with unethical labor practices or environmental violations.

Example: A retailer using SAP EHS could block orders from a teak supplier lacking FSC certification, preventing unsustainable wood from entering their inventory.

2. Reducing Carbon Footprints in Manufacturing and Shipping

Outdoor furniture often involves energy-intensive processes, from welding metal frames to treating weather-resistant fabrics. SAP EHS enables retailers to:

  • Monitor Emissions: Track CO2, water, and waste output at every production stage using IoT sensors integrated with SAP EHS.
  • Optimize Logistics: Calculate the carbon footprint of shipping routes (e.g., sea vs. air freight) and recommend low-emission options for bulky items like pergolas.
  • Circular Economy Reporting: Measure progress toward recycling goals, such as repurposing 50% of manufacturing waste into new products.

3. Transparency Builds Trust: Meeting Consumer Demands

Today’s shoppers want proof of sustainability. SAP EHS helps retailers share their eco-credentials through:

  • Product Passports: Embedding QR codes on furniture tags that link to details like material origins, recycled content, and carbon footprint.
  • Real-Time Dashboards: Sharing sustainability metrics (e.g., "X tons of CO2 saved this year") on websites or in-store displays.
  • Compliance Reporting: Generating audit-ready reports for certifications like B Corp or the EU Ecolabel.

4. The Future: SAP EHS and the Green Garden Revolution

As sustainability regulations tighten (e.g., EU deforestation laws) and consumer expectations evolve, SAP EHS will play a pivotal role in helping garden retailers:

  • Adopt blockchain for end-to-end supply chain traceability.
  • Leverage AI to predict the environmental impact of new designs (e.g., 3D-printed recycled plastic chairs).
  • Partner with carbon offset programs directly through SAP’s ecosystem.

Cultivating a Sustainable Legacy

For garden furniture retailers, sustainability is no longer optional. By leveraging SAP EHS, businesses can ensure every step—from sourcing raw materials to delivering the final product—aligns with planetary and customer values. The result? A greener bottom line, loyal customers, and a lasting positive impact.

entry.title: Lets Talk to the Database - CAP & SQLite Powered by Google Gemini-2.5-Pro
entry.content:
I am sure everyone is doing great !!!

I am glad and excited to share my learning on Talk to Database , I mean just talk to the database using Natural Language powered by Google Gemini-2.5-pro-exp handled by SAP CAP programming.

So what all we have here to achieve this end-to-end ?

  • We have access to Gemini-2.5-pro exprimetnal version available ( PAID ) Get your API Keys to interact through API Call
  • A CAP Application where we have deployed Sales Order header / Line Item / Deliveries Entities Deployed
  • Install SQLITE DB For Data Storage for all the 3 Tables
  • Add some Dummy data into your DB tables So that later we can validate data and interact with the database for fetching requested information and run select statement on top of that.

Test out Gemini API using POSTMAN before you jump into this.

Data Prep:

  • Extracted data from Sales Order Header (VBAK)
  • Extracted data from Sales Line Item (VBAP)
  • Extracted data from Delivery (LIPS)

 Tasks Break Down:

Prompt Prep :

  1. Prepare the prompt and provide enough context to generate SQL
  2. Example  Prompt – Give me number of Sales Order in System
  3. Prompt Specifies --  A Count is required from Sales Order Header table

Context - 

  1. Sales Order Header = SalesOrderH Table
  2. Sales Order Item = SalesOrderItem

Instruction –

`You are a specialized SQL query assistant for a computer store database. Your primary goal is to answer user questions by retrieving data using the available tools.

Database Context:

The key tables you will interact with are:

   'sp_prompts_Delivery': Contains Delivery Data.

   'sp_prompts_SalesOrderHeader': Contains Sales Order Header Data (often referred to as just Sales Orders).

   'sp_prompts_SalesOrderItem': Contains detailed Sales Order Item Data. `

 

Identify Tables :

  1. Develop NodeJS API to extract tables names Specified
  2. Develop NodeJS API to list all the tables:

Example  - http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/my/listTables

     

 Benefit – this will help in finding out extract matching table while building the select query , will help in preparing the right context for model input

Schema Extraction : Develop Nodejs API to extract schema of tables ( Entities ) specified

Extract Schema for any give table input

Example –

http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/my/describeDbTable

Content-Type: application/json

{   "tableName":"sp_prompts_SalesOrderHeader"  }

Benefit: this will help in preparing schema for tables incase we need only few columns or specific column like Sales Order Types / Delivery Types etc.

 

Execute DB Query:  This is the most important one once Model has generated the select statement for the input , this will execute the query and provide the response

Example – Prompt - SELECT * FROM sp_prompts_SalesOrderItem

Response :

Benefit: - It will run the DB Statements and will return the results

 

Prepare Response:

This will come into picture in case we are requesting some information and expect the response in a plain english Language

Example – Give me no of Sales Order and Sales Item from System

If you notice the generated sql then you will get the idea what happening behind the scene.

Generated SQL

CAP Structure:

This is how my CAP Structure look like.

QueryEngine Contains the Main API Call to Gemini which subsequently calls all the functions required to get data back from Database like below.

AI Model Used :  Gemini-2.5-pro-exp

Initialize of GenAI Model like below.

If you see we have passed set of instructions that contains information about the database and tools are nothing the list of function which will give information about Tables and their Schema and later the final function for executing the generated query.

Dbquery.cds

This action will be responsible for prompt handing and it will return object of Information.

Dbquery.JS

This will handle all the incoming requests ---QueryDatabase is the one which will be triggered from outside rest of functions will be called internally , these are listed for standalone testing.

Note

I am also attaching a small Video Clip where you can see the execution of various prompts for technical implementation DM me directly , i can help you in building this POC.

#KeepLearningKeepSharing

entry.title: Re: Duplicate run of integration flow consuming event from event mesh
entry.content: I cannot see your photos because they are tiny, but I would recommend reviewing this blog that covers AMQP. https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/cloud-integration-connecting-to-messaging-systems-using-the-amqp-adapter/ba-p/13419906

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content:
Glad it worked for you.

Re: "how to turn off hyperlink in WinForms (Windows) and allow it in ASP.NET Web?"
The simplest option is to use 2 different rpt file. 

An alternative is to use a function from a UFL (User Function Library) to identify an environment variable, read a registry entry or an ini file setting, get the machine name, etc.  Ken Hamady maintains a listing of 3rd-party UFLs here. Several of them provide functions for some/all these use cases.

entry.title: Updated HRIS Mappings - but HRIS Sync job not updating the users data
entry.content:
Hi all,

Currently using an implementation test tenant, that has over 1200 default users provided by SAP.

I have added some HRIS mappings, so that Custom1, Custom2 should be updated with different data. (e.g. setting placing Standard Weekly Hours into Custom12. See below

However, when I run the HRIS Sync job, the data is not updating for all the users. I would have expected that Custom12 would now be updated with users Standard Hours.

The only way it did eventually sync the mappings was to make a change directly to the user. So I went and changed Standard Hours from 40 to 39. This had a knock on effect, and now for only that user - the custom fields have mapped correctly.

How do I get all users to have their Custom Fields updated?

Thanks,

Ed

entry.title: Re: CAP Fiori Elements warnings on READ requests
entry.content:
Hello,

I haven't updated this post, but the issue seems to be on the Fiori Elements side. Warning functions in the CAP service add the "sap-messages" header as it should. It is probably not implemented in FE to show a notification on READ requests.

entry.title: PaPM Freeze -Transfer Price Issue
entry.content:
We’ve loaded the A25 TP Freeze file and expect the articles to be frozen at the prices specified in the file. However, some articles are showing double the expected transfer price.

For all price lists, some articles have doubled prices.

Could you please provide your inputs?

SAP Profitability and Performance Management SAP Profitability and Cost Management

entry.title: 🇫🇷 Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, and SAP generative AI Hub
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and SAP generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have knowledge about JavaScript as we're using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant. You should also have good knowledge about the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model.

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (genAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you'll become acquainted with the theory behind genAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you'll become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday June 19th, 2025 at VINCI Energies, Saint-Denis, France from 10:00 am - 4:00 pm. The event will be held in english.

The event is kindly being hosted by VINCI Energies , thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

The instructor will be Developer Advocate @KRiedelsheimer .

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Update Number Range Interval->NR Status in SAP HCM from SAP IDM
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

Our Company requirement is that SAP IDM should update the NR Status column for the Number Range Interval in SAP HCM. Appreciate if you could help to confirm whether it can be done or not as we couldn't find any relevant information in the standard SAP IDM documentation.

In case, it can be done, please share the relevant document/URL which provide the details reg this.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Update Number Range Interval->NR Status in SAP HCM fr...
entry.content: A customized script will be required for this process. This is not available within the SAP IDM documentation

entry.title: Re: Create and handle surrogate keys for dimensions in Da...
entry.content: Thanks for the feedback. I have now found this blog entry https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-auto-generate-ids-for-primary-foreign-keys-in-sap-data-warehouse/ba-p/13545310 which shall solve my challenge. In general this works, need to test it in a "real" data flow...

entry.title: 🇫🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Thursday, June 19th in Saint-Denis, France 🇫🇷 from 10:00 to 16:00 CEST. The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is Developer Advocate Nora von Thenen from SAP.

The event is kindly being hosted by VINCI Energies , thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: Re: Support CrystalReportViewer.DoubleClickPage in ASP.NE...
entry.content: Thanks for your great help ido, I will maybe use a report parameter to identify whether its run on Web or Windows...

entry.title: Joined AI SAP CodeJam event, June 19th 2025, with Nora and Kevin
entry.content:
We have something special for you!

VINCI Energies reached out to us with the ask that they wanted to host a double trouble CodeJam which is all about AI. That is where @noravonthenen and I @KRiedelsheimer joined up to execute on two CodeJams at the same time:

  • Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, and SAP generative AI Hub
  • Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

We can host 50 people in total, which makes it 25 each!

Not only will be there for the CodeJams which are happening June 19th, in Saint-Denis 🇫🇷 France but we will also be guests at the SAP Stammtisch event one day prior in Paris on June 18th. Details on the SAP Stammtisch will follow.

If you are interested in joining us for a full day of learning, make sure to follow one of each links to register. Please do not register for both.

See you in Paris!

entry.title: How to Extend a controller extension in custom action controller/ use invokeAction in custom action?
entry.content:
I have created a custom action in Fiori Elements using Page Map and crated a controller file and function also for the action. I have to use invokeAction to a method that added in capm. But the problem is getting error while using invoke action. How can we achive this?

@SAPSupport @christof_mueller @Sandra_Rossi @DominikTylczyn 

"controlConfiguration": {
                "@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem": {
                  "tableSettings": {
                    "type": "ResponsiveTable"
                  },
                  "actions": {
                    "DataFieldForAction::CatalogService.EntityContainer::CreateProductList": {
                      "afterExecution": {
                        "enableAutoScroll": true
                      }
                    },
                    "customCreate": {
                      "press": "productlistfe.ext.controller.CustomCreate.onClickCustomCreate",
                      "visible": true,
                      "enabled": true,
                      "requiresSelection": false,
                      "text": "customCreate",
                      "position": {
                        "placement": "After",
                        "anchor": "DataFieldForAction::CatalogService.EntityContainer::CreateProductList"
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              },
sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageToast"
], function (MessageToast) {
    'use strict';

    return {
        onClickCreateProductListIDCustom: function (oEvent) {
            MessageToast.show("Custom handler invoked.");

            // Create dialog
            var oDialog = new sap.m.Dialog({
                title: "Create Product List",
                content: [
                    new sap.m.Label({ text: "Name", labelFor: "nameInput", }),
                    new sap.m.Input("nameInput", {
                        placeholder: "Enter name"
                    }),
                    new sap.m.Label({ text: "Description", labelFor: "descInput" }),
                    new sap.m.Input("descInput", {
                        placeholder: "Enter description"
                    })
                ],
                beginButton: new sap.m.Button({
                    text: "Submit",
                    press: function () {
                        var name = sap.ui.getCore().byId("nameInput").getValue();
                        var desc = sap.ui.getCore().byId("descInput").getValue();

                        // Setup the parameters for invokeAction
                        let sActionName = "CatalogService.createProductList"; // Fully qualified action name
                        let mParameters = {
						
                            // var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel();
                            model: this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel(), // get the OData V4 model
                            contexts: [], // for unbound actions, pass empty array
                            label: "Submit",
                            parameters: {
                                name: sName,
                                description: sDesc
                            }
                        };

                        MessageToast.show("Submitted: " + name + " - " + desc);
						
                         // Use the Fiori Elements Edit Flow API
                         this.editFlow.invokeAction(sActionName, mParameters)
                         .then(() => {
                             MessageToast.show("Product List Created!");
                         })
                         .catch((error) => {
                             MessageToast.show("Error while creating Product List.");
                             console.error(error);
                         });


                    }
                }),
                endButton: new sap.m.Button({
                    text: "Cancel",
                    press: function () {
                        oDialog.close();
                    }

                }),
                afterClose: function () {
                    oDialog.destroy();
                }
            });

            oDialog.addStyleClass("sapUiResponsiveContentPadding");
            oDialog.open();
        }

    };
});

entry.title: Re: Our client requirement is to create invoice per each handling unit (HU)
entry.content: can you please tell how you did it

entry.title: suppressing header if group sections are suppressed
entry.content:
I have a report with 3 groups, all are suppressed except for the bottom group. (Details are suppressed too).

in the bottom group i have summary data, the bottom group section is suppressed if these summaries don't meet a criteria.

usually i get around 10 lines of data that aren't suppressed but recently i got less, so i'm thinking what to do when i have a report with no viable data (where nothing meets the criteria and every subgroup is suppressed).

In that case i would like the header to also be suppressed, is there any way to do that?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV to retrieve Purchase Or...

entry.title: Re: Sales Order number not populated at the time of Adjus...
entry.content:
You might have solved the problem by now, but in case others are still looking, the solution is to use CONVERT KEY statement in the save method of your BO:

CONVERT KEY OF a_costrevenuereassignment FROM iv_pid TO rs_key.


Note that syntax highlighter of some older (but not the newest ADT 3.48 based on 2502 cloud release) shows KEY with red, as it would be incorrect, but don't let that deter you. However, the documentation in the above ADT is still not updated and says on page for "CONVERT KEY": 

Used to retrieve the final keys (final_key) from preliminary keys  (pre_key) in the context of RAP late numbering. It is only possible between a COMMIT ENTITIES BEGIN... and COMMIT ENTITIES END. statement.


Also note that in my case only the header entity returns a persistent ID. What about the items (more specifically, about their additional key fields)? Just use READ ENTITIES once you get the header key here.

If you also need the messages, there is a method map_messages that can be implemented, but it requires a declaration "foreign entity a_costrevenuereassignment" at BO level. I'm having trouble here, as I can't wrap this declaration as a tier 2 and my entity is not yet released.

entry.title: Re: How to automate clearing status of Bank Items when po...
entry.content:
Hi @Melle1028,

I read the post and understood that you wanted to clear the AP and AR open items while doing the Manual Bank Statement.

Firstly you have selected wrong Manual transactions: F005 and F006 won't work in this case as SAP predefines they accounting entries. Please check below SS

Instead of this you can use F022 and F023 for clearing the open items. Please check below SS for accounting entries

Secondly, while maintaining the Manual Bank Statement under Bank Statement Items you need to enter invoice number in Customer Reference Number field.

This should work and resolve your issue. Please let me know if you have any questions.

entry.title: How to use SOAP API B2B For BOM sales order creation
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

Can I use SOAP API B2B (OrderRequest_In) for Inbound Sales order creation (BOM Order)?

I am getting Pricing procedure cannot be determined Error (012-EDI_SD)

In payload, I am passing <SupplierProductID> SAP Material No under <OrderItem>

Expectation is BOM material will explore in sales order, Note its working fine while create sales order through VA01 tcode. I have enter BOM main material in sales order item, once enter BOM component material showing in subsequent line item.

Thanks,

Arindam

entry.title: Re: How to setup S/4HANA Cloud to get market rates (e.g. exchange rates) automatically
entry.content:
@MARA_FORTES the BYOR doesn't pull rates from anywhere. What you would have to do is push the rates from the ECB (or anywhere else you like) to the SAP Market Rates Management BYOR instance you deploy. For that you will need to deploy some agent somewhere to do that job for you on a regular basis. You could do that with something like Power Automate, a timed Azure function, or many other ways.

Hope that helps,

Joerg.

entry.title: Error while applying Aggregation to a table
entry.content:
SAP Fiori Elements SAPUI5 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model 

I'm trying to make a full stack application on the SAP Business Application Studio using Fiori Object Page Floorplan, and oData v4 service built using Cloud Application Programming. I'm trying to display a table on the object page and show the sum totals of a column in the bottom row. 

To display sum totals at the bottom row I'm using Analytical Table with Aggregation annotations. It is working for one table (WorkOrderItems) but not for the other (RaBillAbstractItems). My guess is that there's probably some problem with expanding the related workOrderItem element in the RaBillAbstractItems. But I have no idea how to solve this.

Here's some screenshots and code snippets of what I've done:

First, here's the working aggregation code (for the workOrderItems Table):

@Aggregation.CustomAggregate#amount : 'Edm.Decimal'
@Aggregation.CustomAggregate#quantity : 'Edm.Decimal'
entity WorkOrderItems as projection on my.WorkOrderItems
{
  *,
  @analytics.Measure
  @Aggregation.default: #SUM
  amount,
  @analytics.Measure
  @Aggregation.default: #SUM
  quantity,
};
//////////////////////////////////////////
annotate RABillsService.WorkOrderItems with @Aggregation.ApplySupported: {
  Transformations       : [
    'aggregate',
    // 'groupby',
    // 'filter',
    // 'search'
  ],
  Rollup                : #None,
  PropertyRestrictions  : true,
  // GroupableProperties   : [rate,  ],
  AggregatableProperties: [
    {Property: amount, },
    {Property: quantity, },
    // {Property: rate, }
  ],
};

Next, here's the code that's giving me error:

@Aggregation.CustomAggregate #raAmount: 'Edm.Integer'
@Aggregation.CustomAggregate #raQuantity: 'Edm.Integer'
@Aggregation.ApplySupported                 : {
 // GroupableProperties   : [to_workOrderItem.rate, ],
 AggregatableProperties: [{Property: raAmount}]
}
entity raBillAbstractItems as projection on my.raBillAbstractItems{
  *,
  @analytics.Measure
  @Aggregation.default: #SUM
  raAmount,
  @analytics.Measure
  @Aggregation.default: #SUM
  raQuantity,
};

Here's my db schema for reference:

entity WorkOrder : cuid, managed
{
    key workOrderNumber : String(100);
    currency : Currency;
    date : Date;
    Items : Composition of many WorkOrderItems on Items.workOrderParent = $self;
    project : Association to one Project;
    raBills : Composition of many raBillAbstract on raBills.parentWorkOrder = $self;
}

entity WorkOrderItems : cuid, managed
{
    activityCode : String(20);
    activityDescription : String(30);
    amount : Integer;
    percentCompleted : UInt8;
    quantity : Integer;
    rate : Integer;
    serialNumber : String(15);
    stage : String(30);
    subPackageName : String(20);
    unitOfMeasurement : String(5);
    workOrderParent : Association to one WorkOrder;
}

entity raBillAbstract : cuid, managed
{
    key billNumber : Integer;
    billDate : Date;
    complianceCompleted : Boolean;
    endDate : Date;
    invoiceNumber : String;
    receiptDate : Date;
    startDate : Date;
    status : BillStatus;
    typeOfBill : BillType;
    Items : Composition of many raBillAbstractItems on Items.raBillParent = $self;
    parentWorkOrder : Association to one WorkOrder;
}

entity raBillAbstractItems : cuid, managed
{
    raAmount : Integer;
    raQuantity : Integer;
    key raBillParent : Association to one raBillAbstract;
    to_workOrderItem : Association to one WorkOrderItems;
}

I've not made any custom pages or controllers yet. I have been using just the annotations to manipulate the front-end. Instead of the analytical table, if you have any documentation for showing sum of columns on responsive table that would work too, as the the "Analytical table" documentation on Fiori mentions this:

Do not use the analytical table (ALV) if:
You need a table. The responsive table is the default table in SAP Fiori. Additional use cases where you might need the responsive table include:
You need to provide a total sum for one column. You can also add totals to the responsive table.

However, I did not find any annotations for how to enable this.

entry.title: Re: Everyone are talking about MCP... What is MCP and how will it help for your process
entry.content:
Hi,

nice intro. Thanks for that.

Is SAP already planning to integrate this capabilities in Joule Studio or Datasphere (generate MCP interface for Datasphere Models).

Beside intrinsic utilization within the SAP universe, this would boost integration e.g. with Microsoft Copilot Studio, which already supports MCP.

Would be nice to see some of that in the various SAP roadmaps.

entry.title: Re: API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV to retrieve Purchase Or...
entry.content:
Dear user,

Thanks for the clarification. Since you're calling API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV from SAP BTP, the short answer is:

Yes, you can retrieve the supplier confirmation quantity using the API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV, but you must use the correct navigation entity:

 

Use the entity: A_PurchaseOrderItemConfirmation

This entity is part of the same service API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV, and is intended to retrieve the confirmed quantities.

 

How to do it in SAP BTP (via OData)

URL Example:

GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV/A_PurchaseOrderItemConfirmation?$filter=PurchaseOrder eq '4500000010'&$format=json

It will return:

  • PurchaseOrder
  • PurchaseOrderItem
  • ConfirmationCategory
  • ConfirmationDate
  • ConfirmedQuantity ← 🎯 Your equivalent of EKET-DABMG
  • ConfirmationControl

 

You can also $expand from PO to Confirmation

Example:

GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV/A_PurchaseOrder?$filter=PurchaseOrder eq '4500000010'&$expand=to_Item/to_Confirmation&$format=json

You’ll get:

  • Header data
  • All items
  • Under each item → the supplier confirmation lines with ConfirmedQuantity

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: SAP BODS passing parameters to ODP
entry.content: I developed a CDS VIEW (ODP object in BODS) with parameters. Now I need to passing the parameter through SAP BODS to execute the ODP. it's possible?

Hello everyone,

I'm experiencing an issue in my SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) story. Recently, I’ve noticed that checkboxes have started appearing next to the objects in Edit mode on the left side panel.
This behavior wasn’t there before, and I’d like to revert back to the original view without these checkboxes.

Does anyone know how I can remove these checkboxes and restore the default display in the outline panel?
Could this be a setting I’ve accidentally enabled, or is it related to a recent update in the SAC interface?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance.

Is there here any config available to redirect to dedicated Inbox by process ?

Thanks for your answer,

Best regards,

Olivier

entry.title: Re: Negative Balances in AR Aging Report Due to FBRA on P...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

You're raising an issue that comes up often in aging report design when FBRA (Reset Cleared Items) and DZ documents (Customer Payments) are involved.

Let’s explore a practical approach for your custom AR aging report in SAP S/4HANA (Public or RISE).

 

Resume of your problem

  • FBRA reverses clearing documents.
  • This reopens historical invoices, but posts the reversal in the current period (e.g., Feb 2025).
  • Retroactive aging reports for 2022 and 2023 now show negative balances due to:
    • Reopened invoices (again aging)
    • DZ (payment) documents still dated in the original cleared year
  • CFO expects aging to show only open items, no negatives, aligned to FS/FBL3N.

 

Key Design Goals

  • The report must:
    • Match financial statements (FS10N/FBL3N)
    • Respect current open items and aging
    • Avoid misleading negative values from historic DZs
    • Support both retrospective and current aging snapshots

 

Recommended Approach

1. Use clearing information dynamically

In your aging logic, leverage the fields:

  • BSEG-AUGDT (Clearing date)
  • BSEG-AUGBL (Clearing document)
  • BSEG-AUGGJ (Clearing fiscal year)
  • BSEG-AUGCP (Clearing posting period)

To determine the item status as of the aging key date (e.g., 31.12.2023):

  • If AUGDT <= Aging Date, treat item as cleared
  • If AUGDT > Aging Date or is blank, treat item as open

This allows simulating the cleared/open status as of any date.

 

2. DZ Documents: exclude or match reversed clearing logic

For DZ documents, which may still appear:

  • Link them to the invoice via clearing documents (AUGBL)
  • Only consider them if cleared before or on the aging date
  • If FBRA reversed the clearing after aging date → the invoice was still cleared at that point → no need to include DZ in aging

 

3. Negative Balances Handling Rule

Add a rule:

  • If the invoice was fully cleared as of aging date → exclude both invoice and payment from aging
  • If only partially cleared by aging date → include remaining balance, but not overpayments (to avoid negatives)

 

4. Use Snapshot Tables (Optional)

For performance or audit purposes, you might:

  • Build and store monthly snapshots of AR open items
  • Helps with consistent retroactive reporting without recalculating logic every time

 

5. Report Enhancements (Optional)

Add flags to show:

  • Was item fully cleared as of aging date?
  • Is DZ reversal impacting retro view?
  • Clearing document reversal info (FBRA detection logic)

This helps users understand edge cases and audit trails.

 

Technical Pointers

  • BSEG is your friend, but in S/4HANA ACDOCA is often preferred.
  • Use ACDOCA-AUGDT and ACDOCA-CLEAR_DATE fields.
  • Consider using CDS Views like:
    • I_JournalEntryItemCube
    • I_CustomerLineItem with custom logic

 

I hope this will help answer your questions.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Batch Number Transactions Report in SAP Business One...
entry.content:
When you say Web Browser, do you mean Web Client (the pretty one) or Browser Access (the one that looks like B1 client)?

Browser Access - yes

Web Client - no

entry.title: collective orders and dependencies
entry.content: With a collective order in released status I need the ability to break the dependency between parent and child orders. For example I may receive and ECN that eliminates a child component from the BOM or possibly a quantity change. What options do I have to "detach" this dependency so that I may make my BOM changes on the parent level order?

entry.title: Where can I start learning advanced planning in SAP S/4HANA using (embedded) PP/DS?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Advanced Production Planning with SAP S/4HANA PP/DS learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey discusses essential features and requirements for advanced production planning in SAP S/4HANA using Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (PP/DS). Also referred to as embedded PP/DS or ePP/DS. You explore the master data requirements for advanced planning, and you work with the alert monitor. The core features of Demand Management and the various (groups of) planning strategies in embedded PP/DS in SAP S/4HANA are explored. Make-to-order production, make-to-stock production, and various related features and functions in embedded PP/DS are discussed. The concept and use of heuristics is explained, as well as various concepts and principles for detailed scheduling, like the finiteness level of a resource and the use of alternative modes. The detailed scheduling planning board is introduced, and you explore the functionality known as Supply Creation-Based Confirmation. Both scheduling optimization and an introduction to production planning optimization (PPO) are also covered.

Learning objectives
By the end of this learning journey, you will be familiar with advanced planning in SAP S/4HANA using (embedded) PP/DS. You will know the master data requirements for PP/DS, and you will have worked with the product view and the alert monitor. You will have seen and executed various planning examples of both make-to-order and make-to-stock production. You will also have worked with detailed scheduling, and you will know about optimization in both DS and in production planning (using PPO).

Prerequisites

  • Exploring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Production Planning

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Inquiry about setting the default values for deprecia...
entry.content:
There is no way to set the field to be active by default, atleast not without an addon.

However, you can set mandatory distribution rule for GL accounts used in depreciation posting. This way user will be forced to check this option in order to post the depreciation.

entry.title: How to Auto-Populate Manager Name in Department Based on Custom Position Field in Successfactors?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a requirement in SAP SuccessFactors where I added two custom fields in the Department (FO_Department) object:

  1. cust_chiefPos – a custom field of type Position (Foundation Object), used to store the manager position of the department.

  2. cust_chiefName – intended to store the name of the user who holds that position.

The goal is:
Whenever a user selects a manager position (cust_chiefPos) in the department, I want the cust_chiefName field to be automatically filled with the name of the user (incumbent) who holds that position.

I encountered issues accessing userNav or the incumbent details in the rule editor.
Also, I'm unsure whether cust_chiefName should be a String, or if it's possible to use User type directly and still auto-populate it.

Any help or best practice is highly appreciated!
Thanks in advance 🙏

TQM

entry.title: Re: A Step-By-Step Walkthrough for SAP Analytics Cloud compass (PART I)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Nicolay_ ,

Thanks for the comments and your detailed blog post too!

In your post the author touched on the necessity of an account dimension or a sufficiently detailed key figure structure for a compass simulation, resulting in the need of a careful model design and preparation. I would like to shed another light on this: The main concept of a Monte Carlo Simulation is actually the approach of using high numbers of repeated calculations with random inputs to introduce the element of uncertainty into an otherwise deterministic calculation. It does not seek to detect latent relationships between mathematically unrelated key figures, and serves a different use case than e.g. the time series forecast. (More details in this blog here.) Hence the existence of a calculation describing the relationship between all key figures involved is a prerequisite. 
Nevertheless, I really like your blog and how you have worked with compass. I find the 7 ideas in your blog for further enhancements and missing components very interesting and would like some more details. But a blog comment is not the best platform for this: Hence I would like to encourage you to create entries for those in the influence portal where we can continue to exchange on the topics. We are already looking into enhancements regarding other aggregations and more flexibility. 

Great to read your/Alex's blog on compass - keep them coming! 🙂

Cheers,
Chen Nee

entry.title: Re: Recursion in SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Hi all,

When using the tail recursion, will the stack be released after every call like in Haskell?

Best regards,

entry.title: 🇫🇷 Joined AI SAP CodeJam event, June 19th 2025, with Nora and Kevin
entry.content:
We have something special for you!

VINCI Energies reached out to us with the ask that they wanted to host a double trouble CodeJam which is all about AI. That is where @noravonthenen and I @KRiedelsheimer joined up to execute on two CodeJams at the same time:

  • Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, and SAP generative AI Hub
  • Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

We can host 50 people in total, which makes it 25 each!

Not only will be there for the CodeJams which are happening June 19th, in Saint-Denis 🇫🇷 France but we will also be guests at the SAP Stammtisch event one day prior in Paris on June 18th. Details on the SAP Stammtisch will follow.

If you are interested in joining us for a full day of learning, make sure to follow one of each links to register. Please do not register for both.

See you in Paris!

entry.title: SAP BTP React app - CSRF token validation failed - during POST to Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm a little but stuck. Hope you can help, here is the case:

I'm currently working on a application which fetches data from the backend, and posts new entries to custom business object using API calls. 

I've wrote the application in React, using SAP BAS to deploy it to SAP BTP on CF. 

I use the xsuaa service, bind it to my app, and have login feature to start the application.

I use a destination service and connect it through the destination to S4Hana Public Cloud with the comm user (yes, I tried oauth2samlbearer but somehow I can't get it to work (anyone got a guide? 😉 ) ). It is bound to the service.

When I fetch data, all works fine, it fetches process order information.

I do some stuff with the orders, and the results should be send to S4Hana Public cloud, to a business object which is exposed. 

So I fetch the X-CSRF-Token (is getting fetched), pass this to the post including payload, and.....it fails with the 403 error that my csrf token validation failed. 

The things I checked already:

The authentication is always set to true, so the cookie handling should be done by the browser:

const getAxiosConfig = () => { return { withCredentials: true, }; };

When I try to do this manually (getting the cookies and combining them), the cookies are not exposed do to security reason. Pretty sure it is not advised to do so btw

export const createProcessOrderSet = async (payload) => {
  const local = "/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_PROCESSORDERS_CDS/YY1_PROCESSORDERS";
  const destination = "/destination/S4H_BACKEND/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_PROCESSORDERS_CDS/YY1_PROCESSORDERS";
  const url = process.env.NODE_ENV === "production" ? destination : local;
  const config = { withCredentials: true };

  // Log URL for debugging
  console.log("Using URL:", url);

  try {
    // Fetch CSRF token via GET request
    const csrfResponse = await axios.get(`${url}?$top=1`, {
      ...config,
      headers: {
        "X-CSRF-Token": "Fetch",
        Accept: "application/json",
      },
    });

    // Retrieve CSRF token using both lower and proper case header names
    const csrfToken = csrfResponse.headers["x-csrf-token"] || csrfResponse.headers["X-Csrf-Token"];
    if (!csrfToken) {
      throw new Error("CSRF Token not received from SAP");
    }
    console.log("CSRF Token:", csrfToken);

    // Rely on the browser's cookie management (withCredentials) for session cookies
    // No manual Cookie header is set here.

    // Perform the POST request using the CSRF token
    const postResponse = await axios.post(url, payload, {
      ...config,
      headers: {
        "X-CSRF-Token": csrfToken,
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "Accept": "application/json",
      },
    });

    return postResponse.data;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(" Error creating process order set:", error);
    throw error;
  }
};

When I do all this locally using a proxy in my app, the posting and stuff just works fine. It looks like the destination is the issue I think, but even that is not something unfamiliar. Any hints or tips regarding the destination or the destination services? I searched of course but notes and hints are mostly targeted at onprem/private cloud. The manual cookies didn't work, no authentication didn't work, the only difference is the destination, but the fetch of data (standard api's) do work, except for the post.

Anything would help 🙂

Cheers!

Laurens

entry.title: Re: Limitation on storage locations
entry.content: @jagdeepsingh83, Regarding you question - this is not our recommendation to do so. But when presenting the options to the customer, we wanted to make sure, that this would be possible from a technical side. (In fact, I would be happy if there was a limitation so that this "option" could be exluded right away...)

entry.title: How to change end date of a work agreement during transfer situation in manage workforce
entry.content:
Rehiring does not work as expected and the end date is not changed.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to change end date of a work agreement during tra...
entry.content:
To change the start date/end date of a work agreement during rehire or transfer situation, follow below steps:

1) Inside worker profile in manage workforce, press 'Edit' button.

2) Choose the work agreement with transfer or rehire scenario and press 'Correct' button.

3) Edit the details as per requirement and save.

entry.title: WFA on Hana Succession Metric: Number of Nomination Deleted Records Showing
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1,

We have successfully managed to only show the latest change in Succession. However, we noticed that the deleted records for the Number of Nomination are showing.

We have checked the measure code and it does or should exclude records with status 3. We have also noticed that the same issue occurs with the Number of Successors.

Any suggestions on how to exclude deleted records from Succession measure counts?

Thank you.

Best regards,

Zaklin Perosa

@mikehoekstra1

entry.title: 360 and performance & potential
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to have the standard performance vs. potential section in a 360 view?

Even if you're copying this section from a non-360 view and pasting it at the XML level?

Has anyone tried this?

entry.title: Re: Limitation on storage locations
entry.content: There is no limitation on how many storage locations you can create—a combination can have more than 1M entries. However, as it is a configuration activity, only the system administrator or a qualified person is allowed to create them. Therefore, it should not be a concern for the end client or user how many Storage locations can be created. This activity can impact your security roles if you restrict anything in users' roles. The customer should not think about going creative with it and creating 2000+ storage locations without having a thoughtful conversation and a business reason.

entry.title: Re: Cannot deploy iFlow due to warning.
entry.content: Even I'm getting the same warning message and not able to deploy the iFlow.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order Item is not matched to Invoice Item in...
entry.content:
Hi Linda,

Even i am unable to get correct result for 3 way match.

In my case PO Qty-100, GR Qty - 100, Invoice Qty - 250, hence you would expect 3- way match error.

However as the scorecard explanation given in the note 3540020 , this is not a 3-way match failure !!!

As, Item description is matching and unit price and currency is matching, which gives 0.5 + 0.35 = 0.85, which exceeds the likeness score of 0.65. Hence, invoice is getting processed automatically.

I believe there should be an ability to re-assign matching score. Item description should have score of 0.25 in my opinion, remaining 0.30 for Qty , 0.30 for unit price , 0.15 for currency.

Regards,

Ashish Shah

entry.title: Re: Issues with Recording Simulations in Microsoft Edge –...
entry.content: I am also seeing this error on 135.0.3179.66 (Official build) (64-bit) with 2503 😢

entry.content: Nice post!

entry.content: But to define a "step user" will be also following the job template or assigning further authorization of business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_APJ_JCE  ?

entry.title: Re: Error to create sales order (error 8B 213)
entry.content: Hello Henrique, hope you're good!

entry.title: IDOC Message's Column is Different on SAP and MII
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In our factory, our planning department creates work orders on SAP and this work orders comes to MII side with IDoc messages. 

IDoc Message Type's name is ZPP_BIAS. And we're using it for 2 years without problem. This month, our SAP department added a new column in ZPP_BIAS IDoc Messages, it's name is KAPTPROG.

When we're creating a new message, column named KAPTPROG is seen on WE02 page. But when this message comes to MII side, this KAPTPROG column is not showing. I added two files to this message which is SAP and MII side XML's.

We checked everything on SAP for any lack of configuration but we couldn't find it. We believe problem is on MII side. On MII, we using SAPMEINT_INBOUND_QUEUE for processing rule for incoming IDoc messages. 

So, any one of you have ever experienced a problem like this ? Or any one of you knows the solution for this problem ? Thanks advance.

Alper

entry.title: Re: Is there a solution to prevent personal email on from...
entry.content:
Hi @ToniaHolmes, Thanks for your question. The display of such data (e.g. email) is controlled via RBP. See guide > prerequisites are:

  • You have the data field defined in the data models for your system.
  • To view the data, employees have the view permission for the corresponding field under User Permissions > Employee Data.

Kind regards, Diana

entry.title: Re: H1_2025 | badge management overall limit per instance
entry.content:
Hi @pboulicaut,

Thanks for your question. Currently 20 different badge types are supported.

Kind regards, Diana

entry.title: The cash flow is not generated automatically
entry.content:
What are the additional settings are required to enable automatic generation of cash flows?

We confirmed that executing the Flow Builder job manually via "Schedule Jobs for Flow Builder" works as expected.
In the Starter System, the cash flow is generated automatically without any issues.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: 🇩🇪 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use embeddings to improve your RAG response and build your own chatbot with memory.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Monday April 28th, 2025 at the SAP AppHaus (HDL02) in Heidelberg, Germany 🇩🇪 from 09:00 - 15:00 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

The instructor will be Developer Advocate @noravonthenen.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: The cash flow is not generated automatically
entry.content:
Configure the source application accounting using the configuration activity 'Define Source Application Accounting' (SSCUI ID 105487), follow these steps:

  • Set the Company Code, Accounting Scope, Cash Position Update Mode, and Liquidity Analysis Update Mode as per requirements.

Update Modes Explanation:

  1. Close To Real Time

    • System monitors FI postings.

    • Newly posted or changed documents are inserted into the staging table.

    • System automatically triggers the FlowBuilder Plus technical job to process these entries.

  2. Deferred - Delta Table Update

    • System monitors FI postings.

    • Newly posted or changed documents are inserted into the staging table.

    • System does not automatically trigger the FlowBuilder Plus job.

    • A custom job needs to be scheduled to trigger processing.

  3. Deferred - No Delta Table Update

    • System does not monitor FI postings.

    • No insertion of posted or changed documents into the staging table.

  4. Not Relevant

    • Default update mode when Accounting Scope is set to Liquidity Analysis Only.

    • No update activities are carried out.

The information is provided in the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3596982

entry.title: Re: Exploring New SQL on Files Use Cases with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024 and QRC 01/2025
entry.content:
@JasonHinsperger Thanks for providing the documentation. The access via Spark seems not the same as using "real" delta sharing as it its promoted for BDC and (SAP) Databricks.

In the article it is mentioned that "read-only access to external Delta Sharing is supported", so I was wondering if also "fully-integrated" delta sharing in the sense of zero-copy sharing (as described here by @Yogesh__Vijay) is possible or planned for HANA Cloud data lake?

entry.title: Re: Multi tab in FIORI always show 0 count
entry.content: Hi,
